2604.24154.title: Progressive Approximation in Deep Residual Networks: Theory and Validation
2604.24154.paper_summary: The Universal Approximation Theorem (UAT) guarantees universal function approximation but does not explain how residual models distribute approximation across layers. We reframe residual networks as a layer-wise approximation process that builds an approximation trajectory from input to target, and prove the existence of progressive trajectories where error decreases monotonically with depth. It reveals that residual networks can implement structured, step-by-step refinement rather than end-to-end (E2E) black-box mapping. Building on this, we propose Layer-wise Progressive Approximation (LPA), a theoretically grounded training principle that explicitly aligns each layer with its residual target to realize such trajectories. LPA is architecture-agnostic: we observe progressive behavior in residual FNNs, ResNets, and Transformers across tasks including complex surface fitting, image classification, and NLP with LLMs for generation and classification. Crucially, this enables ``train once, use $N$ models": a single network yields useful predictions at every depth, supporting efficient shallow inference without retraining. Our work unifies approximation theory with practical deep learning, providing a new lens on representation learning and a flexible framework for multi-depth deployment. The source code will be released unpon acceptance at https://(open\_upon\_acceptance).
2604.24154.paper_summary_zh: 普遍近似定理 (UAT) 保證了普遍函數近似，但並未解釋殘差模型如何在各層之間分配近似。我們將殘差網絡重新框架為一種層級近似過程，從輸入到目標構建近似軌跡，並證明了存在逐步軌跡，其中誤差隨著深度單調減少。這揭示了殘差網絡可以實現結構化的逐步精煉，而不是端到端 (E2E) 的黑箱映射。在此基礎上，我們提出了層級進步近似 (LPA)，這是一種理論上有根據的訓練原則，明確地將每一層與其殘差目標對齊，以實現這樣的軌跡。LPA 是架構無關的：我們觀察到殘差 FNN、ResNets 和 Transformers 在包括複雜曲面擬合、圖像分類和 NLP 中使用 LLM 進行生成和分類的任務中表現出進步行為。關鍵是，這使得「訓練一次，使用 $N$ 模型」成為可能：單個網絡在每一深度都能產生有用的預測，支持高效的淺層推理而無需重新訓練。我們的工作將近似理論與實際深度學習統一，為表示學習提供了一個新的視角，並為多深度部署提供了一個靈活的框架。源代碼將在 https://(open\_upon\_acceptance) 接受後發布。
2604.24154.author: Wei Wang et.al.
2604.24154.authors: Wei Wang, Xiao-Yong Wei, Qing Li
